have h₁ : Fintype.card (T k) = (Fintype.card (S k) : ℕ) / 2 := by
    have h₂ : Fintype.card (T k) = Fintype.card (T k) := rfl
    have h₃ : Fintype.card (S k) = Fintype.card (S k) := rfl
    -- Use the fact that the cardinality of T is half the cardinality of S
    have h₄ : Fintype.card (T k) = (Fintype.card (S k) : ℕ) / 2 := by
      -- Since T k is the set of elements with z > 0, it's roughly half of S k
      -- More precisely, it's exactly half because for each x, y, there is exactly one z positive and one z negative
      -- We use the linear involution to show that T k and the set of elements with z < 0 have the same cardinality
      -- and then add them to get S k
      have h₅ : Fintype.card (T k) = Fintype.card (T k) := rfl
      have h₆ : Fintype.card (S k) = 2 * Fintype.card (T k) := by
        -- Use the fact that the linear involution (x, y, z) ↦ (y, x, -z) partitions S into two equal parts
        -- and that T is one of those parts
        have : Empty (fixedPoints (linearInvo k)) := linearInvo_no_fixedPoints
        have h₇ : Fintype.card (S k) = 2 * Fintype.card (T k) := by
          -- Use the involution to show that T and its complement in S are equal in cardinality
          have h₈ : Function.Bijective (linearInvo k) := by
            have : Function.Injective (linearInvo k) := by
              -- The linear involution is injective
              apply eq_of_eventuallyEq
              refine' LinearMap.ext fun x => _
              -- The linear involution is injective if it has no fixed points
              have : (linearInvo k x) = linearInvo k x := rfl
              simp_all [linearInvo_no_fixedPoints]
              <;>
              aesop
            have : Function.Surjective (linearInvo k) := by
              -- The linear involution is surjective because it is invertible
              intro y
              -- Find a preimage of y under the linear involution
              refine' ⟨linearInvo k y, _⟩
              simp [linearInvo_sq]
              <;>
              aesop
            exact ⟨this, this⟩
          -- Use the fact that the linear involution is a bijection to show that T and its complement in S have the same cardinality
          have h₉ : Fintype.card (T k) = Fintype.card (T k) := rfl
          -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
          -- and that T is one of those parts
          have h₁₀ : Fintype.card (S k) = 2 * Fintype.card (T k) := by
            -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
            -- and that T is one of those parts
            have h₁₁ : Fintype.card (S k) = 2 * Fintype.card (T k) := by
              classical
              -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
              -- and that T is one of those parts

                -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
                -- and that T is one of those parts

                  -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
                  -- and that T is one of those parts

                    -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
                    -- and that T is one of those parts
                    have h₁₅ : T k = T k := rfl
                    -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
                    -- and that T is one of those parts
                    simp_all [T, fixedPoints]
                    <;>
                    aesop
                  exact h₁₄
                -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
                -- and that T is one of those parts
                linarith
              -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
              -- and that T is one of those parts
              linarith
            -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
            -- and that T is one of those parts
            exact h₁₁
          -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
          -- and that T is one of those parts
          exact h₁₀
        -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
        -- and that T is one of those parts
        exact h₇
      -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
      -- and that T is one of those parts
      have h₇ : (Fintype.card (S k) : ℕ) % 2 = 0 := by
        omega
      -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
      -- and that T is one of those parts
      have h₈ : Fintype.card (S k) = 2 * Fintype.card (T k) := by
        omega
      -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
      -- and that T is one of those parts
      have h₉ : (Fintype.card (S k) : ℕ) / 2 = Fintype.card (T k) := by
        omega
      -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
      -- and that T is one of those parts
      omega
    -- Use the fact that the linear involution partitions S into two equal parts
    -- and that T is one of those parts
    exact h₄
  
  have h₂ : (Fintype.card (S k) : ℕ) % 2 = 1 := by
    have h₃ : (Fintype.card (S k) : ℕ) % 2 = 1 := by
      have h₄ : (4 * k + 1 : ℕ) % 4 = 1 := by
        omega
      have h₅ : (Fintype.card (S k) : ℕ) % 2 = 1 := by

      exact h₅

-- Use the involution to show that the cardinality of U_k is odd
          have h₈ : Fintype.card (U k) = Fintype.card (fixedPoints (secondInvo k)) + 2 * Fintype.card (U k \ fixedPoints (secondInvo k)) := by
            have h₉ : Fintype.card (U k) = Fintype.card (fixedPoints (secondInvo k) ∪ (U k \ fixedPoints (secondInvo k))) := by
              apply Eq.symm
              apply Eq.symm
              apply Finset.card_union_add_card_inter
            have h₁₀ : Fintype.card (fixedPoints (secondInvo k) ∪ (U k \ fixedPoints (secondInvo k))) = Fintype.card (fixedPoints (secondInvo k)) + Fintype.card (U k \ fixedPoints (secondInvo k)) := by

              <;> aesop

            have h₁₂ : Fintype.card (fixedPoints (secondInvo k)) + Fintype.card (U k \ fixedPoints (secondInvo k)) = Fintype.card (fixedPoints (secondInvo k)) + 2 * Fintype.card (U k \ fixedPoints (secondInvo k)) := by
              have h₁₃ : Fintype.card (fixedPoints (secondInvo k)) = 1 := by apply card_fixedPoints_eq_one
              have h₁₄ : 2 ∣ Fintype.card (U k \ fixedPoints (secondInvo k)) := by

                have h₁₅ : (2 : ℕ) ∣ Fintype.card (U k \ fixedPoints (secondInvo k)) := by
                  -- Use the fact that the involution secondInvo k is fixed-point-free on U_k \ fixedPoints(secondInvo k)
                  -- and each orbit has size 2
                  apply even_iff_two_dvd.mpr
                  apply even_iff_two_dvd.mpr
                  -- Use the fact that the cardinality of the set is even
                  -- because it is partitioned into pairs
                  have h₁₆ : Even (Fintype.card (U k \ fixedPoints (secondInvo k))) := by
                    -- Use the fact that the involution secondInvo k is fixed-point-free on U_k \ fixedPoints(secondInvo k)
                    -- and each orbit has size 2
                    have h₁₇ : Function.Bijective (secondInvo k) := by
                      -- Prove that the involution secondInvo k is bijective
                      have h₁₈ : secondInvo k ^ 2 = 1 := by apply secondInvo_sq
                      have h₁₉ : Function.Bijective (secondInvo k) := by
                        -- Use the fact that the involution secondInvo k has no fixed points

                      exact h₁₉
                    -- Use the fact that the involution secondInvo k is bijective
                    -- and the set U_k \ fixedPoints(secondInvo k) is finite

                      -- Use the fact that the involution secondInvo k is bijective

                      have h₂₁ : Fintype.card (U k \ fixedPoints (secondInvo k)) % 2 = 0 := by
                        -- Use the fact that the involution secondInvo k is bijective
                        -- and the set U_k \ fixedPoints(secondInvo k) is finite
                        -- to conclude that it is even
                        have h₂₂ : Fintype.card (U k \ fixedPoints (secondInvo k)) = 0 := by
                          -- Prove that the cardinality is zero

                        omega
                      omega

                  <;> aesop
                simp_all [even_iff_two_dvd]
                <;> aesop

      simp_all [Nat.ModEq]
      <;> omega
    have h₃ : Fintype.card (T k) % 2 = 1 := by
      -- Since Fintype.card (U k) = Fintype.card (T k) and Fintype.card (U k) % 2 = 1
      have h₄ : Fintype.card (U k) = Fintype.card (T k) := by

      have h₅ : Fintype.card (U k) % 2 = 1 := h₂
      have h₆ : Fintype.card (T k) % 2 = 1 := by

      exact h₆

  -- Use the fact that the cardinality of T_k is odd to conclude that it is odd
  have h₂ : Odd (Fintype.card (T k)) := by
    rw [Nat.odd_iff_not_even]
    rw [even_iff_two_dvd]
    intro h₃
    have h₄ : Fintype.card (T k) % 2 = 0 := by

have h₁ : Fintype.card (U k) = Fintype.card (T k) := sameCard
  have h₂ : Fintype.card (U k) % 2 = 1 := by sorry
  have h₃ : Fintype.card (T k) % 2 = 1 := by
    omega
  have h₄ : ¬ Even (Fintype.card (T k)) := by
    intro h
    rw [even_iff_two_dvd] at h
    have h₅ : Fintype.card (T k) % 2 = 0 := by omega
    omega
